notesOnRaunoLayout: rauno.me renders each card at 1200x720 source-size, then applies a parent transform that scales it to ~720x432 visible size (~0.6 ratio). The label uses an inverse 1.667x transform so its computed font-size (14px) renders at ~14*0.6*1.667 = 14px on screen.
The label sits ABOVE the card (top: -46.6667px in source-space, which is about -28px in screen-space), NOT inside it.
The card body itself contains only the pixelated hand cursor centered, on a pure white background. No giant typography, no border-radius, no shadow, no padding.

Adjacent cards on rauno.me each have similar covers - they vary only in label text and (for some) in centered display elements. The History of Software Design card is the minimal variant: white + cursor only.

card.interiorContent: single centered pixelated hand cursor (svg/img). nothing else visible.

label.position: absolute, top:-46.67px, left:0 - sits ABOVE the card edge in the grey background gap
label.fontFamily: X, -apple-system, system-ui, Segoe UI, Roboto, ... sans-serif
label.fontSize: 14px (source) - effective ~14px on screen after combined transforms

ourMappingNotes: Our cards are 998px wide @ 78vw, not 1200px. We do NOT have the 1200->720 down-scaling pipeline.
Visually anchor the label at top:~-28px from card top so it sits in the gap above the card (matches rauno).
Use color #6f6f6f (= rgb(111,111,111)) to match the rauno label colour exactly.
Keep our label text 'Projects' (do NOT copy 'History of Software Design').
Keep our existing pixelated cursor SVG - it is already faithful to rauno's.

decisionsForApply.borderRadius: var(--card-radius) (6px) - keep ours; rauno doesn't round but our system does on every card so we keep consistency
decisionsForApply.padding: 0 (move padding logic onto inner positioned elements)
decisionsForApply.labelPosition: absolute, top: -28px, left: 0 (sits in the 32px gap above the card)
